name: Fullingmill Cottage Borehole
units: metres, transcribed verbatim from a typed 1965 log; depth = base of interval below ground level

intervals.verbatim: Drift and fill rockhead at 4.30 m.

annotations: mixed lithology; classed by first-named component (sandstone) - shale interbeds present

intervals.verbatim: Interbedded black shale and white sandstone.
annotations: mixed lithology; classed by first-named component (black shale) - white sandstone interbeds present
mixed lithology; classed by first-named component (shale) - sandstone interbeds present
seam named in typed original; trailing '(L)' correlation letter typed inline after the seam name
black shale component below the seatearth

intervals.verbatim: Shale with plants and ironstone nodules.
annotations: plant remains and ironstone nodules noted in original
seam named in typed original; trailing '(M)' correlation letter typed inline after the seam name

seam_marks.how: named in original: Coal, Hutton seam (L).
named in original: Coal, Ruler Seam (M).

notes: Typed 1965 metric log, single page (BGS registration handwritten 'NZ 24 SE 1/28', 'PAGE NO. 2'; printed page number 6 at foot). Header details: Drilled 1965; Position 'East bank of the River Wear and 10 m south of Fullingmill Cottage, below the Cathedral, Durham City. Grid Reference NZ 27214207'; Height 32.50 m AOD; Direction Vertical; Driller Mr. C. Chaplin, Geology Department, University of Durham; Rig Boyles X-ray; Cores examined by William Hopkins. All 21 intervals fully legible; depth arithmetic cross-checks cleanly at every step (no clerk slips) and the final base of 33.53 m matches the index depth exactly. Two named coal seams: Hutton (interval 8, with typed '(L)' correlation letter) and Ruler (interval 20, with typed '(M)' correlation letter) - these letters appear typed inline in the original, not as later pencil marginalia. Seven 'interbedded'/mixed-lithology intervals were each classed by their first-named (dominant) component with a note, since the schema allows only one class per interval. BGS blue banner and page watermarks ignored as non-log content.
